vision_profile.current_state: Aarav is a Backend Engineer with 6 years of experience in Java, Spring Boot, REST APIs, PostgreSQL, and Redis, seeking to advance his career.
vision_profile.main_blocker: His biggest obstacle is a perceived weakness in system design depth, particularly in distributed systems, Kafka, and AWS deployment patterns, which he fears will hinder his progression to senior roles.
vision_profile.top_motivation: He is highly motivated to own larger engineering decisions and move beyond simply executing assigned tasks, aiming for a significant increase in salary and influence.

milestones.label: M01
milestones.o: You can confidently apply core Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) principles and fundamental design patterns to build robust and maintainable backend services.
milestones.identity_statement: You are laying the bedrock for architectural excellence, transforming your coding experience into structured design wisdom.

scenario.title: Refactoring a Legacy Service
Designing a Flexible Payment Gateway
Code Review for Design Flaws

science.desc: You are tasked with refactoring a monolithic Java service. Identify areas where SOLID principles are violated and propose design improvements.

modules.title: Creational & Structural Patterns
lessons: Singleton Pattern: Use cases and pitfalls
Factory Method and Abstract Factory Patterns
Builder Pattern for complex object creation

scenario.title: Designing a Cross-Platform UI Factory
Integrating a Third-Party API
Evaluating Design Pattern Trade-offs

milestones.label: M02
milestones.o: You can design complex APIs, model data effectively, and implement advanced behavioral patterns for flexible and extensible systems.
milestones.identity_statement: You are evolving from a pattern user to a pattern architect, crafting elegant solutions for intricate problems.

modules.title: Behavioral Design Patterns
lessons: Template Method Pattern for algorithm skeletons
State Pattern for object behavior changes

scenario.title: Designing a Workflow Engine
Adding New Features to an Existing System
Identifying Pattern Opportunities

science.desc: You need to design a notification system that supports multiple channels (email, SMS, push) and allows new channels to be added easily without modifying existing code. Which behavioral patterns would you use?

modules.title: API Design & Service Layer
lessons: RESTful API principles and best practices
GraphQL vs REST: Choosing the right approach

scenario.title: Designing a Public API for a SaaS Product
Implementing OAuth2 for Microservices
Migrating API Consumers to a New Version

milestones.label: M03
milestones.o: You can articulate the fundamental components of large-scale systems, including networking, data storage, and their interactions.
milestones.identity_statement: You are expanding your view from individual services to the grand architecture, understanding the interconnectedness of complex systems.

scenario.title: Analyzing Requirements for a Ride-Sharing App
Calculating Storage for a Photo Sharing Service
Choosing Consistency Model for a Distributed Database

science.desc: Design the core components of a new social media platform. What are the key functional and non-functional requirements, and how would you prioritize them?

modules.title: Networking & Communication
lessons: DNS: how it works and optimization
RPC vs. REST for inter-service communication

scenario.title: Troubleshooting Network Latency Issues
Choosing a Load Balancer Strategy
Reducing Latency for Global Users

modules.title: Databases & Storage
lessons: SQL vs. NoSQL: choosing the right database
NoSQL Databases: Key-Value, Document, Column-Family, Graph
Data Warehousing and OLAP vs. OLTP
Storage Solutions: Block, File, Object Storage

scenario.title: Designing a Database for a Real-Time Analytics Platform
Implementing Sharding for a User Database
Designing a Multi-Layer Caching System

milestones.label: M04
milestones.o: You can design and implement robust distributed systems, manage inter-service communication, and leverage messaging queues for asynchronous processing.
milestones.identity_statement: You are mastering the art of coordination in a distributed world, ensuring seamless communication and resilience across services.

modules.title: Distributed Systems
lessons: Challenges of Distributed Computing (CAP Theorem revisited)

scenario.title: Designing a Distributed E-commerce Order Processing System
Designing a Real-time Data Pipeline with Kafka
Migrating a Monolith to Event-Driven Microservices

science.desc: Design a distributed logging and monitoring system for a fleet of microservices. How would you handle log aggregation, real-time analytics, and fault tolerance?

modules.title: Microservices Architecture
lessons: Monolithic vs. Microservices: pros and cons
API Gateway and Backend for Frontend (BFF)
Data Management in Microservices (Database per Service)

scenario.title: Decomposing a Monolith into Microservices
Implementing a Centralized API Gateway
Debugging a Performance Issue Across Services

milestones.label: M05
milestones.o: You can design highly scalable and performant systems, optimize resource utilization, and implement robust cloud deployment strategies.
milestones.identity_statement: You are becoming a master of efficiency and elasticity, building systems that can grow and adapt to any demand.

modules.title: Scalability & Performance
lessons: Caching at different layers (Client, CDN, Application, Database)

scenario.title: Scaling a High-Traffic News Website
Optimizing Database Queries and Application Code
Designing a Thread-Safe Data Processing Pipeline

science.desc: A popular online gaming platform experiences sudden traffic spikes during peak hours, leading to service degradation. Propose a scalable architecture to handle millions of concurrent users.

modules.title: Cloud & Deployment Patterns
lessons: AWS Core Services Overview (EC2, S3, RDS, Lambda)

scenario.title: Designing a Highly Available Application on AWS
Deploying a Microservice Application with Kubernetes
Designing a Cost-Effective Image Processing Service with Lambda

milestones.label: M06
milestones.o: You can design and implement systems that are highly available, fault-tolerant, easily monitored, and secure against common vulnerabilities.
milestones.identity_statement: You are fortifying systems against failure and threats, ensuring trust and continuous operation.

modules.title: Reliability & Observability
lessons: Logging Best Practices and Centralized Logging

scenario.title: Designing a Disaster Recovery Plan for a Critical Service
Setting Up Comprehensive Monitoring for a Production System
Implementing a Chaos Experiment to Test Service Degradation

science.desc: A critical backend service frequently experiences outages during deployments. Design a strategy to improve its reliability, including monitoring, alerting, and rollback mechanisms.

scenario.title: Identifying and Mitigating XSS and SQL Injection
Designing an Authentication Flow with JWT
Configuring IAM Roles and Policies for Least Privilege

milestones.label: M07
milestones.o: You can confidently design complex, large-scale systems, articulate design decisions, and excel in senior-level system design interviews.
milestones.identity_statement: You are now a seasoned architect, ready to lead and innovate, shaping the future of engineering decisions.

scenario.title: Designing a Scalable Notification Service
Implementing a Circuit Breaker Pattern
Performing a Peer Review of a System Design Document

modules.title: Capstone & Career Readiness
lessons: Navigating Career Growth as a Senior Engineer

scenario.title: Writing a System Design Proposal for a New Feature
Whiteboard Session: Designing a Distributed Cache
Making a Build vs. Buy Decision for a Critical Component

science.desc: You are asked to design a global, highly available, and fault-tolerant payment processing system. Walk through your design choices, trade-offs, and scalability considerations.
